overview.description: Research group at CIIRC CTU Prague focused on machine perception, robot learning, and AI for life sciences
overview.description_long: IMPACT (Intelligent Machine Perception) is a research group at the Czech Institute of Informatics, Robotics and Cybernetics (CIIRC) of Czech Technical University (CTU) in Prague. Created in 2023 as a continuation of the EU-funded Intelligent Machine Perception project (2017–2023), the group pursues two main research themes: (1) machine perception and robot learning — developing intelligent systems that perceive dynamic environments, interact with them, and learn from those interactions — and (2) AI for protein engineering and drug discovery, including protein dynamics, enzyme characterisation, and molecule discovery from mass spectrometry data. The group publishes regularly at top venues including CVPR, NeurIPS, ICLR, and Nature Biotechnology.
overview.tagline: Intelligent systems that perceive, interact with, and learn from dynamic environments

history.origin_story: Started in 2017 as the Intelligent Machine Perception project at CIIRC CTU, co-funded by the EU Structural and Investment Funds (Operational Programme Research, Development and Education, CZ.02.1.01/0.0/0.0/15_003/0000468). Reconstituted in 2023 as an independent research group under Josef Šivic's ERC Advanced Grant.

key_milestones.event: Intelligent Machine Perception project launched at CIIRC CTU with EU Structural Funds
Josef Šivic awarded ERC Advanced Grant 2022 (FRONTIER project) for research on learning in dynamic 3D environments
IMPACT reconstituted as independent research group at CIIRC CTU
Work on Open-Vocabulary 3D Occupancy Prediction accepted to NeurIPS 2023
Paper on generating changes of object states accepted to CVPR 2024; NeurIPS 2024 spotlight for molecule discovery benchmark (top 2% of submissions)

Papers accepted to ICLR 2025 on protein flexibility engineering and 6D object trajectory extraction; Nature Biotechnology paper on molecular representation learning (DreaMS); Nature Catalysis paper on quantum computing for enzyme discovery

financials.parent_company: Czech Technical University in Prague (CIIRC CTU)

leadership.name: Jiří Sedlář
leadership.role: Head of Group (AI for Life Sciences)

PhD Student (Computer Vision / ML / Robotics / AI for Life Sciences)

awards: CLARA Center partner (EU Horizon, neurodegenerative diseases / AI + supercomputing)

Inria Paris — Willow and Sierra teams
CIIRC CTU Applied Algebra & Geometry group (Tomas Pajdla, Torsten Sattler)

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: Academic research group pushing the frontier of computer vision, robotics, and AI for life sciences. Not a commercial product — AGI commoditization is not relevant to its mission.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: University research group funded by EU grants and ERC; not subject to commercial market dynamics.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: Supported by a prestigious ERC Advanced Grant (2022/2023) and embedded within CIIRC CTU with multiple EU project participations (ELISE, euROBIN, ELIAS, FRONTIER); funding is grant-based and multi-year.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: Academic research lab with strong grant funding, high-impact publications at top venues, and institutional affiliation with Czech Technical University. Not a commercially exposed entity.

metadata.notes: Listed as 'company' in companies_index.json but is in fact a university research group at CIIRC CTU. No CNAIP URL available. Category should likely be 'research_group'. ERC Advanced Grant 2022 awarded to PI Josef Šivic for project FRONTIER. Original 2017-2023 project co-funded by EU Structural Funds (CZ.02.1.01/0.0/0.0/15_003/0000468). Alumni have gone on to Meta FAIR Paris, Mistral AI, Inria Paris, Google Research.
